title: Guard implicit await across run scopes
goal: Implement a small issue #13 scoping guard so implicit swarm await_members does not wait across multiple active run_id scopes owned by the same coordinator.
permissions: Modify swarm tool await scoping and tests
Run cargo fmt/check/test and selfdev build
completionConditions: await_members without session_ids, target_session, or run_id infers the sole active owned run_id when exactly one non-terminal owned run has active workers.
await_members without explicit scope returns an actionable error when multiple active owned run_id values are present, instead of sending an unscoped request that waits across runs.
await_members keeps existing server fallback when there is no single active owned run_id and no mixed active runs.
Focused helper tests, cargo fmt/check, git diff --check, selfdev build/reload, governance, commit, and push pass.
failureTaxonomy: Breaking explicit session_ids or target_session await behavior
Treating terminal/failed/stale workers as active run ambiguity
Adding blocking network or provider calls beyond existing CommList lookup
notes: Limit to tool-side inference/error before CommAwaitMembers. Do not redesign SwarmRun persistence in this slice.
